meta.target_buyer: Project Manager / Partner at Planning Firms

score.label: Proceed with Caution
score.interpretation: Proceed with Caution — validation and urgency gaps

diagnosis.headline: Homepage is well-built — but urgency is missing and proof shows activity, not outcomes
diagnosis.text: This is a strong homepage. Category is clear, pain is specific, alternatives are named, and there's a low-commitment entry point. The gaps are in urgency (no cost of waiting) and validation (testimonials lack role titles, metrics show activity not results). Buyers will respond but deals may drift without urgency framing, and champions will struggle to justify spend without ROI ammunition.

reply_rate.basis: Based on Q3 (pain specific) + Q6 (ICP clear)

demo_conversion.basis: Based on Q17 (free trial) + Q13 (process visible)

internal_sell.basis: Based on Q12 (vanity metrics) + Q22 (no ROI)

pipeline_speed.basis: Based on Q5 (no urgency) + Q20 (pricing hidden)

predictions.summary: Buyers will respond and convert to trials. Deals may slow when partners ask 'what's the ROI?' or 'why not stick with current tools until we're busier?'

recognition: What does this actually cost? We need to know if this fits our budget before a demo.
Our current tools work okay. What would we actually save by switching?
Do you have planning firms like us — same size, same project types?
We're not launching a project right now. Can we look at this when we win the next RFP?

message_priority.evidence: Hero leads with outcome ('Turn digital community engagement into a competitive advantage') and pain is explicit in the Problem section. Buyers immediately see relevance.

buyer_focus.evidence: '100+ planning firms' is specific. The problem framing (fragmented tools, setup time, overengineered platforms) speaks directly to planning firm workflows.

why_act_now.evidence: Pain is named but no cost of waiting. No 'every manual project costs you X hours' or timing pressure. Buyers can defer to next quarter.

proof_relevance.evidence: Named testimonials exist (Dr. Julian Petrin, Michael Frehn) but no role titles. Metrics (250+ projects, 400k+ participants) show activity, not outcomes. No 'saved X hours per project.'

explanation_burden.evidence: 'Community Engagement Platform for Planning Projects' is immediately clear. Category is recognizable. No invented terminology.

internal_sharing.evidence: If a project manager forwards this to a partner, the partner will see process but not ROI. No economic buyer ammunition. Pricing requires a conversation.

mixed_signals.evidence: Consistent SaaS motion throughout. 'Try for free' matches the product-led approach. GDPR and data ownership addressed in FAQ. No contradictions.

breakpoint.insight: Buyers understand the value and want to believe, but the proof doesn't give them ammunition. Testimonials lack role titles (is Dr. Julian Petrin a partner? project manager?). Metrics show platform activity (250+ projects) not customer outcomes (hours saved, margin recovered). When the partner asks 'what will this actually save us?' the project manager has no answer.

missing_items.text: Outcome metrics: hours saved per project, margin improvement, time-to-report
Role titles on testimonials: Partner vs. Project Manager vs. Planner
Indicative pricing: budget qualification without booking a call
Cost of inaction: what manual engagement actually costs

decisions.name: Why Act Now
decisions.finding: Pain is clear but no cost of waiting. 'Every RFP without structured engagement is a competitive disadvantage' or 'Manual consolidation costs X hours per project' would create urgency.

decisions.name: Results Achieved
decisions.finding: '250+ projects' and '400k+ participants' show platform scale, not customer outcomes. Where's 'saved X hours per project' or 'Y% faster from feedback to report'?

decisions.name: Role-Matched Proof
decisions.finding: Testimonials have names and companies but no titles. 'Dr. Julian Petrin, Partner, urbanista' would be stronger than 'Dr. Julian Petrin, urbanista'.

decisions.name: Pricing Visibility
decisions.finding: 'Discuss pricing based on your project volume' means buyers can't self-qualify. Even 'Starting at €X/project' or tier names would help budget conversations.

recommendations.eyebrow: Light Refinement — Not a Major Rebuild
recommendations.action: This homepage is already strong. Four targeted additions would close the gaps:

goals.label: Add Urgency Framing
goals.description: Quantify the cost of manual engagement. 'Every project without structured engagement costs X hours of consolidation' or 'RFPs without digital methodology win Y% less often.'

goals.label: Swap Vanity for Outcome Metrics
goals.description: Replace '250+ projects' with customer outcomes: '60% less time from feedback to report' or 'X hours saved per project on average.'

goals.label: Add Role Titles to Testimonials
goals.description: 'Dr. Julian Petrin, Partner' or 'Michael Frehn, Project Lead' — let buyers see proof from their role.

goals.label: Show Indicative Pricing
goals.description: Even 'Starting at €X per project' or tier names (Starter/Professional/Enterprise) would help budget conversations happen before the demo.

evidence.question: What is this?
evidence.quote: Community Engagement Platform for Planning Projects
evidence.inference: Clear category. Immediately understood.

evidence.question: What do you do?
evidence.quote: Run map based participation, structure feedback automatically and deliver report ready results
evidence.inference: Core function clear in hero.

evidence.question: Pain worth switching?
evidence.quote: Fragmented tools slow projects down / Setup and analysis consume too much time
evidence.inference: Specific pain. Recognizable to planning firms.

evidence.question: What project?
evidence.quote: Upload your RFP to generate a ready to use engagement platform
evidence.inference: Entry point tied to buyer's task (RFP response).

evidence.question: Why act now?
evidence.quote: Not addressed
evidence.inference: No cost of waiting. No timing pressure. Buyers can defer.

evidence.question: For my team?
evidence.quote: Trusted by 100+ planning firms
evidence.inference: Clear ICP. Segment is specific.

evidence.question: For my situation?
evidence.quote: Most planning firms can set up and launch their first project within hours
evidence.inference: Time-to-value clear. FAQ addresses various situations.

evidence.question: Switching from?
evidence.quote: Project website. Survey tool. Map software. Excel analysis. Slide reporting.
evidence.inference: Alternatives explicitly named.

evidence.question: Why alternatives fail?
evidence.quote: Each project becomes manual stitching / Existing platforms are overengineered
evidence.inference: Weaknesses of alternatives clear.

evidence.question: What's different?
evidence.quote: One platform for the entire workflow / Smart automation from setup to reporting
evidence.inference: Differentiation clear: integrated vs. fragmented.

evidence.question: Teams like mine?
evidence.quote: urbanista, Planersocietät, urban policy + logo bar
evidence.inference: Named companies. Planning firms visible.

evidence.question: Results achieved?
evidence.quote: 250+ planning projects / 400k+ participants / 94% client retention rate
evidence.inference: Activity metrics. No outcome metrics (hours saved, margin).

evidence.question: How much effort?
evidence.quote: set up and launch their first project within hours, not weeks
evidence.inference: Time-to-value clear.

evidence.question: Proof match role?
evidence.quote: Dr. Julian Petrin, urbanista (no title)
evidence.inference: Names but no role titles. Is this a partner or project manager?

evidence.question: Enterprise signals?
evidence.quote: GDPR-compliant, German servers / Data security and privacy are fundamental
evidence.inference: Public sector compliance addressed in FAQ.

evidence.question: First step?
evidence.quote: Try for free / Book demo
evidence.inference: Multiple entry points. CTA clear.

evidence.question: Safe to try?
evidence.quote: Upload your RFP... Try for free
evidence.inference: Low-commitment entry. No credit card needed implied.

evidence.question: After I click?
evidence.quote: Senf generates a ready to share... platform
evidence.inference: Immediate value promise. What happens next is visible.

evidence.question: CTA match readiness?
evidence.quote: Try for free + Book demo
evidence.inference: Product-led + sales-assisted. Good coverage.

evidence.question: Offer match motion?
evidence.quote: Discuss pricing based on your project volume
evidence.inference: SaaS motion but no visible pricing. Buyers can't self-qualify.

evidence.question: Explore without sales?
evidence.quote: Explore the platform / 6-step workflow / video previews
evidence.inference: Process visible. Videos exist. No ungated guide.

evidence.question: ROI documentation?
evidence.quote: Recover margin on every project (claim, no proof)
evidence.inference: ROI promised but not documented. No calculator or case study.

indicators.what: 'What does this cost?' asked before demo
indicators.direction: Should decrease after pricing visibility added
Should improve as outcome metrics replace vanity metrics

indicators.what: Time from first touch to signed contract
Champion can explain ROI to partner
indicators.direction: Should improve after ROI documentation added
